title: achillea formosa subsp. formosa
intro: Achillea formosa subsp. formosa, also known as Western Yarrow, is a perennial herb in the sunflower family native to Western North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which help to treat wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and ease digestive issues. The health benefits of Western Yarrow include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing skin irritations. Western Yarrow can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in folk remedies and natural health practices.
